From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Zum zum zum n° 2](/w/index.php?title=Zum_zum_zum_n%C2%B0_2&redirect=no "Zum zum zum n° 2"))

1969 Italian film

| Zum zum zum nᵒ 2 | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Directed by | [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") |
| Starring | [Peppino De Filippo](/wiki/Peppino_De_Filippo "Peppino De Filippo") [Little Tony](/wiki/Little_Tony_(singer) "Little Tony (singer)") [Lino Banfi](/wiki/Lino_Banfi "Lino Banfi") |
| Release date | * 1969 (1969) |
| Country | Italy |
| Language | Italian |

***Zum zum zum nᵒ 2*** is a 1969 [musicarello](/wiki/Musicarello "Musicarello") film directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci"). It is the sequel to the film *[Zum zum zum - La canzone che mi passa per la testa](/wiki/Zum_zum_zum_-_La_canzone_che_mi_passa_per_la_testa "Zum zum zum - La canzone che mi passa per la testa")*.

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zum_zum_zum_n%E1%B5%92_2&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

Tony, who is to marry Rosalia, falls in love with the beautiful Valeria and chaos ensues.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zum_zum_zum_n%E1%B5%92_2&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Little Tony](/wiki/Little_Tony_(singer) "Little Tony (singer)") as Tony
* [Isabella Savona](/w/index.php?title=Isabella_Savona&action=edit&redlink=1 "Isabella Savona (page does not exist)") [[it](https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isabella_Savona "it:Isabella Savona")] as Rosalia
* [Dolores Palumbo](/wiki/Dolores_Palumbo "Dolores Palumbo") as Tosca
* [Paolo Panelli](/wiki/Paolo_Panelli "Paolo Panelli") as Omino Col Singhiozzo
* [Peppino De Filippo](/wiki/Peppino_De_Filippo "Peppino De Filippo") as Peppino Bertozzini
* [Eva Thulin](/wiki/Eva_Thulin "Eva Thulin") as Valeria
* [Orietta Berti](/wiki/Orietta_Berti "Orietta Berti") as Suor Teresa
* Walter Brugiolo as Carletto Bertozzini
* [Enzo Cannavale](/wiki/Enzo_Cannavale "Enzo Cannavale") as Filippo
* [Nino Terzo](/wiki/Nino_Terzo "Nino Terzo") as Filiberto Caputo
* [Carlo Delle Piane](/wiki/Carlo_Delle_Piane "Carlo Delle Piane") as Nando
* [Stelvio Rosi](/wiki/Stelvio_Rosi "Stelvio Rosi") as Gianni
* Elvira Tonelli as Donna Laura
* [Valeria Sabel](/wiki/Valeria_Sabel "Valeria Sabel") as Stefania
* [Luca Sportelli](/wiki/Luca_Sportelli "Luca Sportelli") as Sor Ignazio
* [Lino Banfi](/wiki/Lino_Banfi "Lino Banfi") as Pasquale
* [Pippo Baudo](/wiki/Pippo_Baudo "Pippo Baudo") as himself
* [Bruno Canfora](/wiki/Bruno_Canfora "Bruno Canfora") as himself

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zum_zum_zum_n%E1%B5%92_2&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Reception")]

The film received poor critical response. A review from *[Avvenire](/wiki/Avvenire "Avvenire")* described the film as "a cloying alternation of ditties, sentimental intrigues, and comic sketches" in which "a reassuring cartoonish banality triumphs on all fronts".[[1]](#cite_note-1) An Italian review of the time found the screenplay "elementary” and the filming and acting ’”inappropriate”[[2]](#cite_note-2) [Paolo Mereghetti](/wiki/Paolo_Mereghetti "Paolo Mereghetti") described the film as "very weak", with [Little Tony](/wiki/Little_Tony_(singer) "Little Tony (singer)") not credible in his role and a certain repetitiveness of situations already shown in the first film.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
